title: service-accounts — helper functions
description: Two global helpers. Everything else the module exposes is behind DI / facades — helpers here are for the rare case where a downstream module needs a synchronous lookup without pulling in the repository binding.

helpers.purpose: ULID lookup returning the resolved ServiceAccount model (or null when not found). Runs through the `svc:lookup:id:{service_account_id}` cache. Does NOT expose credential material — the caller may only read model attributes classified `internal`.
behaviours: Returns null on unknown id (never throws — caller decides how to signal)
Returns null when the SA is soft-deleted (retention hold in effect)
Does NOT decrement the rate-limit counter — that's the middleware's job
Never logs the SA id at INFO level; only at DEBUG level with structured fields

helpers.purpose: Convenience accessor. When a request has been authenticated by service_account.verify_jwt, returns the corresponding ServiceAccount row. Returns null for requests not authenticated via SA (User, PlatformUser, or unauthenticated).
behaviours: Delegates to the RequestContext binding populated by service_account.verify_jwt
Cached in the request-scoped container — repeated calls in one request don't hit the DB
Returns null on Web-worker / CLI contexts unless the CLI is running inside a SA-authenticated wrapper

notes: Helpers are registered by the composer autoloader from `src/Support/helpers.php`. Namespaced functions are safe to call across every framework layer including migrations + seeders.
Neither helper accepts a raw secret — secret verification lives entirely inside `SecretRotator::verify()` on the exchange-endpoint path.

There is deliberately NO `verify_service_account_jwt(string $token): ?ServiceAccount` helper — that lives on `ServiceAccountJwtIssuer::verify()` behind the DI binding so unit tests can mock it cleanly.
